input_name: Starter motor main feed solenoid trigger and ground return
location_visible: Starter motor and solenoid area on engine/gearbox side
identified_function_or_status: Starter high-current B+ feed plus solenoid trigger region and ground return evidence
existing_wiring_reconciliation: Reconcile to HD1 heavy cable path plus ignition/start control and WP03B starter-interrupt path; include engine/chassis ground in the same voltage-drop check
refit_action: Replace damaged or taped small-wire ends; clean and protect heavy lugs; label solenoid trigger; confirm engine-to-chassis ground strap contact before final wrap
verification_before_connection: Read solenoid terminal markings; continuity-test start trigger from key/start relay path; crank-test solenoid pull-in; voltage-drop-test B+ and ground while cranking
closeout_evidence_required: Labelled before/after photo plus cranking voltage-drop readings
notes: Do not rely on old splice colors; prove terminal identity at the starter

input_name: Alternator charge output regulator exciter sense and ground
location_visible: Alternator body and rear terminal side
identified_function_or_status: Alternator charging circuit and regulator/exciter/sense wiring evidence
existing_wiring_reconciliation: Reconcile to main charge lead plus charge warning/excite circuit plus voltage sense if fitted plus any external regulator wiring in the existing master
refit_action: Map actual terminal markings before terminating; replace taped joints and weak ring or plug terminals; add heat/oil protection and strain relief near alternator
verification_before_connection: Confirm alternator terminal labels on the unit; continuity-test to battery/fuse point and warning lamp; live-test charge voltage and lamp behavior after start
closeout_evidence_required: Labelled terminal photo plus charging-voltage log
notes: Do not assign B/L/IG/S/E or similar functions from wire color alone

location_visible: Injection-pump/throttle-linkage side with nearby hard lines
identified_function_or_status: Electrical input near injection-pump or throttle linkage; possible fuel stop idle-up or engine-control function
existing_wiring_reconciliation: Reconcile to WP03B fuel-stop/diesel cutoff only after the device and wire behavior are proven; manual stop cable remains the backup path
refit_action: Label connector and branch; replace damaged plug/boots if retained; secure loom away from linkage and injector lines; leave final wrap open until running tests pass
verification_before_connection: Identify device by terminal count and mechanical action; continuity-test to loom/dash side; test key-on/key-off behavior; verify normal engine shutdown plus manual fallback
closeout_evidence_required: Function label photo plus key-on/run/key-off test result
notes: This is the likely place where the new diesel cutoff plan may meet existing engine hardware but it is not assigned until tested

input_name: Engine sender branch to gauges or warning lamps
location_visible: Engine side sender/wire branch with green/red wiring visible
identified_function_or_status: Probable sender or switch branch for gauge or warning-lamp input
existing_wiring_reconciliation: Reconcile to existing gauge/warning branch in WP03 and the dash cluster wiring map after sender type is identified
refit_action: Trace each sender wire; replace brittle terminals and boots; protect the branch from heat/oil; record sender terminal labels
verification_before_connection: Resistance-test sender if gauge type; continuity-test to dash endpoint; ground-switch-test if warning-lamp type; confirm correct dash response
closeout_evidence_required: Sender close-up plus dash response test result
notes: Treat as probable sender evidence only; exact coolant/oil/other function must be proven at the component

location_visible: Engine or gearbox-side inline connector close-up
identified_function_or_status: Two-wire connector present but function unassigned from photo
existing_wiring_reconciliation: Hold as unassigned until both connector sides are traced; reconcile only after component endpoint and key-state behavior are known
refit_action: Tag temporarily as U-01; inspect locking tabs and seals; repair or delete only after function is confirmed
verification_before_connection: Pin-to-pin continuity; voltage check in OFF/ACC/RUN/START; component endpoint identification; insulation check to ground
closeout_evidence_required: Photo with temporary label plus continuity record

location_visible: Loose connector/wire branch near injection-pump/engine-front area
identified_function_or_status: Loose engine connector present but endpoint and function unassigned
existing_wiring_reconciliation: Hold as unassigned until traced to component or confirmed as obsolete/nonstandard wiring
refit_action: Tag temporarily as U-02; trace loom direction; replace damaged shell if retained; remove only if proven non-baseline and safely isolated
verification_before_connection: Continuity to harness endpoint; key-state voltage check; component-end inspection; comparison against existing pin map
closeout_evidence_required: Photo with temporary label plus repair/delete decision
notes: Keep this separate from EEI-003 until the endpoint is proven

input_name: Engine input loom route and protection
location_visible: Injection-pump side hard-line and linkage route context
identified_function_or_status: Routing and clearance control for engine input branch
existing_wiring_reconciliation: Reconcile to Electrical Master final protection row and any harness clip/bracket decisions before final wrap
refit_action: Use P-clips or retained brackets plus abrasion sleeve plus heat/oil resistant protection plus grommets plus strain relief; keep branches serviceable
verification_before_connection: Check clearance to throttle linkage plus injection lines plus alternator plus starter plus steering/exhaust heat paths plus service-tool access after engine movement allowance
closeout_evidence_required: Final routed photo set with labelled clips and sleeves
notes: This is not a circuit by itself; it is the closure gate that prevents the identified inputs becoming future faults

input_name: Fuel level sender and rear loom gauge input
location_visible: Rear cargo floor and fuel tank bay context; sender close-up still missing
identified_function_or_status: Fuel tank level sender circuit; not an engine input but belongs in the same electrical sender closeout list
existing_wiring_reconciliation: Reconcile to L3 rear loom sender branch and instrument-cluster fuel gauge input; distinguish fuel-level sender from rear lighting and any future camera or display wiring
refit_action: Capture tank sender or pickup close-up; tag sender lead; inspect tank ground path; do not close rear loom until gauge sweep is proven
verification_before_connection: Identify sender terminal and ground or reference path; resistance-test sender sweep; continuity-test to dash cluster; bench or live-test gauge response before final loom wrap
closeout_evidence_required: Sender unit close-up plus fuel gauge sweep/result log
notes: Added from owner note 2026-05-18; current photo refs are tank and cargo-floor context only not sender terminal evidence
